# UC-11.3.63 · Proofpoint TAP Threat Status False-Positive Review Queue

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Governance &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.*

---

## Description

Threats marked `falsePositive` in TAP SIEM exports show analyst or engine corrections—tracking them in Splunk supports tuning, quality review, and audit evidence for email security operations.

## Value

Quantifies how often classifications are overturned, helping managers justify rule changes and training while documenting review cadence.

## Implementation

`threatStatus` may appear under `threatsInfoMap` arrays; expand multivalue fields if needed. Exclude cleared test campaigns via sender allow list. Export monthly summaries to GRC when required. Coordinate field names with Proofpoint support if using custom parsers.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint_tap_siem" earliest=-30d
| eval ts=coalesce(threatStatus, ThreatStatus, "")
| where ts="falsePositive"
| stats count by classification, threatId
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (classification × threatId), timechart of false-positive volume, pie by threat family.

## Known False Positives

Analyst over-ride queues during tuning; a growing queue is not always a false positive.

## References

- [Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3681)
